activities.title: Core Marxist Concepts in Action
activities.activity_preamble: This activity builds your ability to use key Marxist concepts accurately by observing how an expert applies them before trying yourself.

required_materials.specification: Concise exposition defining capitalism, surplus value, class struggle and alienation with one short applied example for each; depth_floor: L3
Step-by-step explanation of analysing a short workplace scenario using Marxist concepts (identify class, identify surplus value, explain exploitation); depth_floor: L3
Model paragraph showing correct use of at least three concepts applied to a scenario; depth_floor: L3
Checklist with at least 4 items: correct use of terms, accurate definitions, appropriate application to example, clarity of explanation; include revise guidance if not met; depth_floor: L3

activities.learner_task:
1. Read the concept explanations.
2. Study the worked example and sample output.
3. Apply the same structure to a new short scenario (provided in the text).
4. Write a 120-word explanation using at least three concepts.
5. Use the checklist to review and revise.
activities.expected_output: A short written explanation correctly applying at least three Marxist concepts to the scenario, revised using checklist feedback.

activities.title: How Capitalism Generates Inequality and Crisis
activities.activity_preamble: You will analyse how key mechanisms produce outcomes by moving from guided reasoning to your own structured analysis.

required_materials.specification: Explanation of exploitation, capital accumulation, and overproduction with causal links to inequality and crisis; depth_floor: L3
Modelled analysis showing cause-effect chain from exploitation to inequality in one scenario; includes intermediate reasoning steps; depth_floor: L3

| Mechanism | What happens | Resulting outcome |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Exploitation | Example partially filled | Inequality |
| Capital accumulation |  |  |
| Overproduction |  |  |
Learner completes missing cells; includes one exemplar row; depth_floor: L3
Checklist (≥4 items): clear causal links, accurate mechanism description, correct outcome, logical chain; includes revision instruction; depth_floor: L3

activities.learner_task:
1. Read explanation and study worked example.
2. Complete the analysis table linking mechanisms to outcomes.
3. Write a short explanation (100 words) explaining one full causal chain.
4. Check your work using the checklist.
activities.expected_output: Completed analysis table and a short causal explanation showing how one mechanism produces inequality or crisis.

activities.title: Comparing Marx’s Predictions with Reality
activities.activity_preamble: This activity develops your ability to compare theory with real-world outcomes using structured evidence.

required_materials.specification: Two short cases: (1) Post-war welfare state in Europe; (2) 2008 financial crisis; each includes brief factual context; depth_floor: L3

| Marx prediction | Actual outcome | Matches? (Yes/No/Partial) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Rising inequality |  |  |
| Increasing crises |  |  |
| Worker immiseration |  |  |
Includes prompt hint column for first row; depth_floor: L3
Checklist (≥4): accurate comparison, use of evidence, clear judgement, correct identification of mismatch or alignment; include revise instruction; depth_floor: L3

activities.learner_task:
1. Read the two scenarios.
2. Complete the comparison table.
3. Write a short paragraph explaining one example where Marx was correct or incorrect.
4. Use the checklist to refine your answer.
activities.expected_output: Completed comparison table and a short paragraph evaluating alignment between prediction and outcome.

activities.title: Was Marx Right? Final Evaluation
activities.activity_preamble: You will bring together your analysis to make and justify your own judgement about Marx’s relevance today.

required_materials.specification: Criteria for evaluation: explanatory power, predictive accuracy, contemporary relevance; depth_floor: L3

| Criterion | Evidence for Marx | Evidence against Marx | Judgement |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Explanatory power | partial example |  |  |
| Predictive accuracy |  |  |  |
| Contemporary relevance |  |  |  |
Includes one partial exemplar; depth_floor: L3
Short essay template (150–200 words) with structure: claim, evidence, counterpoint, final judgement; scaffold only; depth_floor: L3
Checklist (≥4): clear judgement, balanced evidence, use of criteria, coherent argument; revise guidance included; depth_floor: L3
Prompt asking learner to apply Marxist evaluation lens to a modern digital economy example; requires brief justification using same criteria; depth_floor: L3
Learner writes ≥80 words summarising key insights: what Marx explains well, where he is limited, and how to evaluate theories; depth_floor: L3

activities.learner_task:
1. Review evaluation criteria.
2. Complete the decision table.
3. Write your final judgement using the template.
4. Use the checklist to improve your answer.
5. Respond to the transfer prompt.
6. Write a short consolidation summary.
activities.expected_output: A structured evaluative judgement (150–200 words), completed decision table, transfer response, and consolidation summary.

delivery_notes.consolidation_requirement: Final activity includes learner-written consolidation summary
